name: Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular
name_original: Cigudosa y Aguilar del Río Alhama desde Castilruiz — circular por el río Alhama
description: Cycle 21.1 miles (34.0 km) on this moderate road route from Castilruiz, gaining 2194 feet (669 metres) along the Alhama river.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Castilruiz offer varied terrain within the Soria province of Castile and León. The region is characterized by rolling hills and quiet rural roads, providing a peaceful cycling environment. Cyclists can expect to encounter agricultural landscapes and green areas, with routes often featuring significant elevation gains. The area provides a mix of moderate and challenging paths suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Castilruiz The most popular road cycling route is Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop, a 41.7 miles (67.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs and scenic views of the Alhama Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The ascent San Felices from Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular by al Castillo de Magaña de Los, a difficult 36.5 miles (58.7 km) path. This route includes a substantial ascent and passes by historic castle ruins. Local road cyclists also love the Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular, a 21.1 miles (34.0 km) trail leading through rural villages and river valleys, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Road cycling around Castilruiz is defined by rolling hills, quiet rural roads, and varied elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Castilruiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Castilruiz?
A: Road cycling around Castilruiz, located in the Soria province of Castile and León, offers a diverse landscape. You can expect a mix of rolling hills and more challenging mountainous sections, providing varied elevation profiles. The routes often traverse quiet rural roads, agricultural landscapes, and green areas, including forests, allowing for a peaceful and scenic cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Castilruiz?
A: Yes, the region around Castilruiz is known for its challenging road cycling routes. Out of the 10 routes listed, 9 are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077372286/magana-castle-and-alhama-valley-biosphere-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.7-mile route with challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Castilruiz?
A: While the area is predominantly known for its more challenging routes, there is one moderate route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 21.1-mile trail that leads through rural villages and river valleys, offering a less strenuous option suitable for those looking for a moderate ride.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes near Castilruiz?
A: Cyclists in this region will experience a variety of natural features. Routes often pass through extensive green areas, including oak forests, and follow river valleys like the Alhama. The landscape is characterized by rolling hills and agricultural fields, offering picturesque views of the countryside and a fresh, natural environment.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions to see while cycling around Castilruiz?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical and cultural sites. While cycling, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of la Nava del Marqués de Magaña&lt;/a&gt;. The broader Soria province also features historic towns and villages with traditional architecture, and is part of the Romanesque Soria route, showcasing rich cloisters and splendid capitals. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical point of interest nearby.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: The varied terrain of the Soria province, with its rolling hills and mountainous sections, naturally offers numerous scenic viewpoints. Routes often provide expansive views of the Alhama Valley, agricultural landscapes, and green areas, allowing cyclists to immerse themselves in the natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Castilruiz are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077372286/magana-castle-and-alhama-valley-biosphere-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Castilruiz?
A: The best time for road cycling in Castilruiz generally aligns with spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder conditions, especially in higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before planning your ride.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Castilruiz?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Castilruiz is a small rural village. Cyclists typically find parking in or near the village center or at the start points of routes, especially on quiet rural roads. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: Cycling through the rural areas around Castilruiz provides opportunities for rural tourism. While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route are not guaranteed, many routes pass through or near small villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalebreras&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ólvega&lt;/a&gt;, where you might find local establishments for refreshments. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks.

Q: Are the road cycling routes around Castilruiz suitable for families?
A: Given that 9 out of 10 routes are classified as difficult with significant elevation gains, most road cycling routes directly around Castilruiz may not be ideal for families with young children or those seeking very easy, flat rides. The single moderate route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt;, might be more suitable for families with older, experienced cycling members.

Q: How long are the typical road cycling routes in Castilruiz?
A: The road cycling routes around Castilruiz vary in length, catering to different endurance levels. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt; is about 21 miles (34 km), while longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075667186/tierra-de-agreda-loop-via-cigudosa-and-anamaza-river-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Ágreda Loop via Cigudosa and Añamaza River Valley&lt;/a&gt; extend to over 46 miles (75 km).
tips: This small town in the Tierras Altas region is located in the northeast of the province, its term is bathed by the Alhama river, which in turn is a tributary of the Ebro. When you pass the road that comes from Castelruiz, you can see the town of cigudosa
A small stone bridge crossing the Alhama River in the small rural town of Cigudosa.
The road that connects the towns of San Felices with Aguilar del Río Alhama takes you along a narrow road that winds before descending to the second town mentioned.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the 'Cigudosa y Aguilar del Río Alhama desde Castilruiz — circular por el río Alhama' route?
faqs.answer: The starting point of this road cycling route is conveniently located next to a parking lot in Castilruiz, making access straightforward for cyclists arriving by car.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of firm, paved tracks, making it suitable for road bikes. However, be aware that there might be some unpaved or more challenging segments along the trail. The area is characterized by low-altitude mountains and the presence of the Alhama river.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy views of the picturesque village of Cigudosa and cross the Alhama River Stone Bridge near Cigudosa. The route also passes by the Road leading to San Felices, offering scenic views in the foothills of the Sistema Ibérico mountain range.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is approximately 34.0 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 670 meters. It is estimated to take around 1 hour and 50 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this route are not provided, the La Rioja region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder weather, especially in the low-altitude mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate'. While it's not excessively long, the 670 meters of elevation gain requires a good level of physical fitness. Beginners with some cycling experience and good stamina might find it challenging but rewarding.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route on paved tracks, it is generally not recommended for dogs, especially if they are running alongside the bike due to traffic and hard surfaces. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or basket, and always keep them on a leash when off the bike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the 'Cigudosa y Aguilar del Río Alhama desde Castilruiz — circular por el río Alhama' route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama. While specific establishments are not detailed, these villages are likely to offer local cafes or restaurants where you can refuel or take a break.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of several other paths, including the Camino verde del Alhama, Camino de la Vega, Camino del Sabinar, Travesía Virgen de Valvanera, Camino Barranco, Camino de Cigudosa, and Subida a la Ermita.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Given the elevation gain, consider bringing extra layers depending on the weather.
